network.overall_strategy: This network is centered on the only experimentally demonstrated prebiotic synthesis of histidine: the Shen-Miller-Oró route from D-erythrose and formamidine to imidazole-4-acetaldehyde, followed by Strecker conversion and hydrolysis to histidine. To satisfy network completeness while remaining faithful to the literature, the graph includes multiple convergent precursor-generating branches for erythrose and HCN/formamidine, plus parallel imidazole-forming chemistry (Debus-Radziszewski, DAMN/AICN/AICA, and FoDHA-CN-derived imidazole chemistry) as explicitly contextual side branches rather than validated direct histidine routes. Thus the 10 pathways are 10 distinct network traversals that terminate at histidine only when they pass through the Shen bottleneck intermediate imidazole-4-acetaldehyde; other branches feed precursor pools, environmental context, or biochemical capture/stabilization. The design emphasizes literature-backed bottlenecks, hydrothermal-to-surface feedstock transfer, surface concentration and photochemistry, and biochemical preservation/polymerization after histidine formation.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal CO2 reduction to formaldehyde-equivalent feedstock

conditions.other: alkaline vent conditions; feedstock-level reduction step; formaldehyde assignment is a network simplification for carbonyl transfer into surface chemistry

reactions.mechanism: Mineral-mediated CO2 hydrogenation under reducing hydrothermal conditions generates reduced C1 carbonyl chemistry that can be transferred into surface pools.
reactions.reasoning: Greigite and magnetite catalyze CO2 reduction in alkaline hydrothermal systems, with maximum reported activity near 75°C; the deep report supports greigite-mediated CO2 reduction but does not specifically demonstrate formaldehyde as the dominant product. This step is therefore a necessary but explicitly speculative feedstock-bridging abstraction connecting vent carbon reduction to surface carbonyl chemistry, grounded in iron-sulfur CO2 reduction studies and acknowledged as weaker than downstream Shen chemistry (Lilova et al., 2020; Kitadai, 2015; critique context in Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Surface formaldehyde dimerization to glycolaldehyde

conditions.other: shallow pools with UV exposure; glycolaldehyde treated as plausible surface product and sugar precursor

reactions.mechanism: Reduced C1 carbonyls undergo dimerization/photochemical coupling to furnish glycolaldehyde, which is stabilized in borate-containing surface settings.
reactions.reasoning: The deep report identifies glycolaldehyde as a highly plausible prebiotic intermediate and precursor to erythrose, while borate minerals stabilize reactive sugars. The exact mineral-catalyzed route from formaldehyde to glycolaldehyde is not the main focus of the report, so this is a conservative connecting step supported by the general prebiotic abundance of glycolaldehyde and borate-mediated sugar stabilization rather than a single dedicated experiment (Ricardo et al., 2004; Yi et al., 2023; summarized plausibility in the deep report).

reactions.name: Aldol/formose extension of glycolaldehyde to D-erythrose

conditions.other: mildly alkaline pH 8-9; wet-dry concentration in shallow pools; borate suppresses sugar degradation

reactions.mechanism: Glycolaldehyde participates in formose/aldol chemistry to generate tetroses including erythrose; borate complexes stabilize accumulated tetrose products.
reactions.reasoning: D-erythrose is explicitly identified in the deep report as a moderate-plausibility but unstable formose product and direct precursor in the Shen histidine synthesis. Its generation from glycolaldehyde self-condensation at pH 8-9 and stabilization by borate minerals is directly supported in the literature cited in the report (Yi et al., 2023; Ricardo et al., 2004; Decker et al., 1982 as discussed by Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: HCN concentration and formal conversion to formamidine pool

conditions.other: UV-exposed evaporitic pools; concentrated NH3/HCN microenvironments; product is highly labile

reactions.mechanism: HCN-derived amidine chemistry in concentrated, intermittently drying surface pools furnishes transient formamidine available for immediate downstream condensation.
reactions.reasoning: The deep report states formamidine is derived from HCN + NH3 but emphasizes its severe hydrolytic lability and the implausibility of the 0.3 M concentration used in the Shen experiments. This reaction is therefore included strictly because the Shen route requires formamidine, and it is flagged as a weak but necessary precursor-pool step rather than a robustly demonstrated geochemical process (Shen et al., 1987; Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Amadori-type imidazole ring construction to imidazole-4-acetaldehyde

conditions.catalyst: borate minerals and montmorillonite as concentrating/stabilizing surfaces
conditions.other: aqueous solution; formamidine concentration near 0.3 M in the experimental demonstration; Schiff base formation followed by Amadori rearrangement

reactions.mechanism: D-erythrose forms a Schiff base with formamidine, then undergoes Amadori rearrangement/internal redox to build the imidazole ring and furnish imidazole-4-acetaldehyde.
reactions.reasoning: This is the key literature-validated bottleneck step in the only demonstrated prebiotic histidine synthesis, producing imidazole-4-acetaldehyde in 1.6% yield from erythrose. The report highlights this step as uniquely important and chemically parallel to modern HisA-catalyzed ring rearrangement. Mineral agents here are assigned as plausible concentrating/stabilizing surface phases to satisfy the network schema; the experimental papers themselves emphasize aqueous chemistry rather than a required mineral catalyst (Shen et al., 1987; Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Strecker conversion of imidazole-4-acetaldehyde to histidine aminonitrile

conditions.catalyst: surface concentration by sulfide minerals and montmorillonite
conditions.other: mildly basic aqueous Strecker conditions; imine formation followed by cyanide addition

reactions.mechanism: Imidazole-4-acetaldehyde condenses with ammonia to an imine; cyanide adds to give the corresponding alpha-aminonitrile.
reactions.reasoning: The second stage of the Shen-Miller-Oró synthesis proceeds by classical Strecker chemistry from imidazole-4-acetaldehyde, HCN, and NH3 to the histidine aminonitrile intermediate en route to histidine. This is directly supported as part of the sole complete prebiotic histidine synthesis (Shen et al., 1990; summarized in Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of histidine aminonitrile to histidine

conditions.other: aqueous hydrolysis in prebiotic pools; overall reported histidine yield 3.5% relative to erythrose in the Shen route

reactions.mechanism: Nitrile hydrolysis converts the Strecker aminonitrile into the alpha-amino acid histidine.
reactions.reasoning: This final hydrolysis step completes the only experimentally demonstrated prebiotic histidine synthesis, with product identity confirmed by HPLC and LC-MS/MS in the original work. The deep report emphasizes that this route, despite poor plausibility of some inputs, remains the sole complete synthesis known (Shen et al., 1990; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Debus-Radziszewski imidazole synthesis from glyoxal, formaldehyde, and ammonia

conditions.catalyst: TiO2 and montmorillonite as mineral surfaces in mild aqueous pools
conditions.other: mild aqueous room-temperature conditions; acidic microenvironments permit diimine intermediates

reactions.mechanism: Glyoxal, formaldehyde, and ammonia condense through diimine-type intermediates to generate the imidazole core.
reactions.reasoning: The deep report identifies the Debus-Radziszewski reaction as a robust, prebiotically plausible route to imidazole under mild aqueous conditions. It is included here as a contextual imidazole-forming branch and not as a demonstrated route to histidine, because the report explicitly states that the bottleneck is functionalization to imidazole-4-acetaldehyde, which this chemistry does not solve (Oró et al., 1984; Kua et al., 2011).

reactions.name: HCN tetramerization to DAMN

conditions.catalyst: surface concentration on TiO2 and sulfide minerals
conditions.other: HCN-rich UV-exposed pools; oligomerization to the tetramer

reactions.mechanism: HCN oligomerizes under concentrated surface conditions to form diaminomaleonitrile.
reactions.reasoning: DAMN is identified in the deep report as a highly plausible HCN oligomerization product and precursor to aminoimidazole photochemistry. This branch is included because it creates dense network connectivity around HCN and imidazole chemistry, but it is not represented as a validated direct histidine route (Ferris and Orgel, 1966; Ferris and Kuder, 1970).

reactions.name: UV photoisomerization of DAMN to AICN and hydrolysis to AICA

conditions.catalyst: UV-active TiO2 with aqueous mineral interface
conditions.other: UV irradiation near 350 nm; subsequent aqueous hydrolysis of nitrile to carboxamide

reactions.mechanism: DAMN undergoes near-quantitative UV photoisomerization to AICN; aqueous hydrolysis furnishes AICA.
reactions.reasoning: This reaction follows the well-established HCN photochemical route described in the deep report. AICN and AICA are evolutionarily relevant aminoimidazole intermediates linked to purine chemistry, but not demonstrated histidine precursors; their inclusion reflects literature-guided contextual chemistry rather than an invented histidine shortcut (Ferris and Orgel, 1966; Ferris and Kuder, 1970; Vázquez-Salazar et al., 2018).

reactions.name: Formamide-derived imidazole cascade through FoDHA-CN

conditions.other: pH 9.2 cyanide cascade; high-yield imidazole derivative formation from simple precursors

reactions.mechanism: Glycolaldehyde-, cyanide-, and formamide-derived chemistry generates N-formyldehydroalanine nitrile, which reacts with cyanide in a cascade forming an imidazole derivative through multiple C-N bond-forming steps.
reactions.reasoning: The deep report highlights the 2023 Sutherland-group discovery as an especially promising high-yield route to imidazole derivatives, but explicitly notes that extension to histidine or imidazole-4-acetaldehyde has not been demonstrated. This branch is therefore included as a literature-supported exploratory imidazole source, not as a target-producing shortcut (Green, Xu, and Sutherland, 2023).

reactions.name: Mineral-assisted condensation of histidine to histidylhistidine

conditions.other: wet-dry cycling on mineral surfaces such as pyrite, antigorite, or aragonite; stabilization of histidine against decomposition

reactions.mechanism: Concentrated histidine self-assembles on mineral surfaces and undergoes dehydration-condensation to peptide-linked products.
reactions.reasoning: The deep report cites direct evidence that histidine self-assembles and forms peptide bonds on mineral surfaces, increasing its thermal persistence. This downstream biochemical capture step is important because histidine is thermally labile and likely required stabilization after formation. The specific mineral-promoted behavior is supported experimentally, although this step is not part of histidine synthesis itself (Madrigal-Trejo et al., 2021; White and Erickson, 1981 for catalytic significance of histidyl peptides).

pathways.name: Canonical hydrothermal-to-surface Shen route
pathways.description: Vent-derived reduced carbon feeds formaldehyde and glycolaldehyde pools, which generate D-erythrose; HCN and NH3 provide transient formamidine; Shen Amadori chemistry and Strecker hydrolysis yield histidine.

pathways.name: Borate-stabilized erythrose route
pathways.description: A surface-emphasized route in which glycolaldehyde is extended and stabilized by borate minerals to D-erythrose before entering the Shen bottleneck sequence to histidine.

pathways.name: HCN-rich concentrated-pool Shen route
pathways.description: A route emphasizing transient concentration of HCN and NH3 into formamidine, converging with preformed erythrose for the only validated ring-functionalization step toward histidine.

pathways.name: Erythrose-first completion route
pathways.description: Assumes erythrose is already available from surface sugar chemistry and follows only the experimentally demonstrated completion sequence from erythrose plus formamidine to histidine.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-feedstock plus biochemical capture route
pathways.description: A full network traverse from hydrothermal reduced carbon through surface Shen chemistry to histidine, followed by biochemical peptide capture that may preserve the labile amino acid once formed.

pathways.name: Formaldehyde-assisted erythrose accumulation route
pathways.description: Uses hydrothermal formaldehyde input and surface aldol chemistry to reinforce erythrose production before passing through the Shen bottleneck and Strecker conversion to histidine.

pathways.name: Parallel imidazole-context route with Shen convergence
pathways.description: Runs Debus-Radziszewski imidazole formation in parallel with the validated erythrose/formamidine branch, illustrating that imidazole formation is easy but histidine still requires convergence on imidazole-4-acetaldehyde.

pathways.name: HCN photochemistry context with Shen completion
pathways.description: HCN oligomerization to DAMN and AICN/AICA is tracked as a parallel surface branch sharing the HCN hub while the only actual histidine-producing steps still pass through formamidine and imidazole-4-acetaldehyde.

pathways.name: Sutherland-imidazole context with Shen completion
pathways.description: A high-yield cyanide-imidazole cascade provides a modern literature-supported imidazole branch, but target production still requires entry into the Shen bottleneck sequence for histidine.

pathways.name: Maximum-convergence histidine route
pathways.description: A network-dense traversal combining hydrothermal carbon reduction, surface sugar chemistry, HCN-derived precursor accumulation, and contextual imidazole chemistry before the sole validated histidine-forming sequence and downstream peptide capture.
